source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call)
notes: Spec asserts: a synchronous slash command renders locally with no model call, so state stays ready with no →working transition for the slash itself. For codex (verified live against codex-cli 0.130.0) /status writes ZERO lines to the rollout — even cleaner than claudecode, which appends a <local-command> wrapper its parser must filter. The recipe issues a warm-up turn FIRST so the rollout materializes (codex only creates rollout-*.jsonl once the first user message lands; without the warm-up resolve_transcript hangs). After warm_turn_end returns to ready, the slash_window phase pins ready with duration_at_least_ms:4000 and invariants forbidding any state_transition to working/waiting — those are what would fire if /status (incorrectly) drove a turn.

text: Warm-up prompt drives ready → working (real LLM turn; materializes the rollout so the driver can resolve the transcript)
Warm-up turn completes back to ready
Spec: state stays ready throughout /status — no working transition driven by the synchronous slash command (codex writes no rollout line for it)
